what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment: it maps an attachment id to a design-source channel image target (image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-01) and records the attachment type and creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and added timestamp
target uses a custom image:// URI scheme pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-01
No body content beyond the frontmatter
File name is derived from the attachment id
standouts: Custom image:// URI target linking to a pitch image resource
Attachment record is typed as images and tied to the design-source channel
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
File naming matches the attachment id
References external resources by URI rather than embedding them
concerns: No description, title, or alt text for the image attachment
Target URI scheme is not documented within the file
No author or provenance beyond timestamp
notes: This is a minimal attachment index card that points the design-source channel at a specific pitch image resource.
questions: Is the target image versioned or mutable?
Are there other attachment types or metadata fields expected for images?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment record linking a channel to an image resource, which matches its content; it is minimal and does not include descriptive or provenance metadata that might also be expected.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a lightweight, reference-based attachment system where channel memories can point to external image assets without embedding them.
novelty: Use of a custom image:// URI scheme to address pitch images within a design-source channel.
contribution: Provides a stable, id-addressable attachment record that links a channel memory to an external image asset via a typed URI, enabling loose coupling between textual channel content and media files.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record named att-80e6eefc in the design-source channel that references the image resource image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-01 with type images and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment so channel memory can refer to an external pitch image asset by a stable id and URI.
